howItWorks.intro: This cocktail dilution calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Water Added = Pre-Dilution Volume x Dilution Rate
Final ABV = (Spirit oz x Spirit ABV%) / Total Volume
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does ice dilute a cocktail?
faq.answer: Stirring adds 15-20% dilution (about 0.5-0.75 oz per drink). Shaking adds 25-30% (about 1 oz). The dilution is intentional — it opens up flavors and integrates ingredients. Dave Arnold measured this extensively.

faq.question: What ABV should a finished cocktail be?
faq.answer: Classic cocktails target 18-25% ABV. A Martini is about 28-32% (very spirit-forward). A Margarita is about 15-18%. Long drinks (Highball, G&T) are 8-12%. Below 10% and the drink can taste watery; above 30% it may seem harsh.
